topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sheer volume of crumpled papers on the kitchen counter made Leo’s jaw clench. He stared at the mountain of homework—math worksheets, history essays, even a surprisingly detailed diagram of the solar system—and felt a tight knot forming in his chest. "Seriously, Mom?" he muttered, pushing a stray strand of hair from his forehead. He’d been expecting a tidy desk, a system, anything other than this chaotic sprawl. He picked up a crumpled physics assignment, the cheap paper tearing slightly under his grip. It was just… excessive. He sighed, the sound heavy and defeated, and began the tedious process of sorting through the mess, a deep sense of exasperation simmering beneath his skin.

[story 2]

"I swear, this is a prank," Clara said, her voice tight as she surveyed the overflowing filing cabinet in the spare room. Every single graded paper from the last five years was stacked haphazardly, a veritable avalanche of scholastic obligation. Her shoulders slumped, and she let out a slow, shaky breath that seemed to carry all the frustration she was bottling up. She ran a hand over her face, feeling a distinct heat rising in her cheeks. *Why couldn't she just throw them out?* she thought bitterly. She picked up a geometry test, the ink smudging under her fingertips, and shoved it back into the pile with unnecessary force.

[story 3]

Mark stood in the hallway, the smell of old paper and stale pencil shavings thick in the air. He watched his mother methodically arrange notebooks by date, a process that seemed utterly pointless given the sheer quantity involved. He crossed his arms tightly over his chest, his posture stiffening with mounting annoyance. "Mom, we've been over this. We don't need everything archived," he stated, his tone flat and clipped. He took a step back, creating a small distance between himself and the towering collection of academic work. The way he tapped his foot against the linoleum floor betrayed the rising tension in his frame.
